jobs.title: Director, CEO Communications
jobs.body:
MongoDB is looking for a Director, CEO Communications to support the CEO across key communications moments for the company. This is a highly strategic, hands-on individual contributor role responsible for shaping and executing a clear, consistent narrative across customers, investors, and employees.

The person in this role will help connect the dots across quarterly earnings, company All Hands, flagship customer events such as .local, and other key leadership moments. They will work closely with the CEO, CMO, the CEO’s Chief of Staff, Investor Relations, and cross-functional partners to ensure our messaging is aligned, credible, and grounded in the business.

This is not a traditional communications manager role. We are looking for someone who can move quickly from strategy to draft to execution, operate with sound judgment in high-visibility moments, and serve as a trusted partner to the CEO. The right candidate is a strong writer, practical operator, and visual storyteller who is comfortable working across functions and audiences with speed, precision, and low ego. This person must be able to distill complex product roadmaps and financial data into a conversational narrative without losing technical accuracy.

The ideal candidate brings experience from high-growth, venture-backed, or founder-led environments and understands how to pair strong executive communications with external narrative-building.

Key Responsibilities

  • Partner closely with the CEO to shape and execute communications across internal, customer, and investor audiences
  • Help develop and maintain a clear, consistent narrative across key company moments, including earnings, All Hands, .local, and other executive engagements
  • Draft speeches, scripts, talking points, presentations, internal communications, and other executive content in a voice that is authentic to the CEO
  • Partner with creative teams and roll up your sleeves to translate complex ideas into compelling visual narratives, including keynote decks, executive presentations, and digital content
  • Work across the CMO, Chief of Staff, Investor Relations, Corporate Communications, Events, Brand, and Product Marketing teams to align messaging and execution
  • Translate company strategy, product momentum, and market context into clear, compelling, and audience-appropriate communications
  • Partner with Corporate Communications, Investor Relations, and external agencies to help shape and amplify MongoDB’s executive and corporate narrative across priority external audiences and moments
  • Support the CEO in real time before and during major moments, including preparation, iteration, and follow-through
  • Adapt messages for different audiences, including employees, customers, analysts, and investors, while maintaining consistency in the core story
  • Bring speed, discipline, and strong judgment to a fast-moving environment with…

jobs.title: Director, Corporate Development & Ventures
jobs.body:
MongoDB is hiring a Director of Corporate Development & Ventures to partner with senior leadership to shape and execute our inorganic growth strategy, including leading critical M&A and strategic investment activity.

This role is for someone who has operated as a deal lead, not just a participant. You will be expected to independently originate, evaluate, and execute transactions—quickly, rigorously, and with sound judgment.

You will work closely with senior leaders across Product, Engineering, and Finance to inform decisions that shape MongoDB’s position in data infrastructure and the AI ecosystem. There is no room for passive execution in this role—you are expected to drive outcomes.

Finally, you will build the corporate development capability to run multiple transactions in parallel, establishing the cross-functional cadence and playbooks required to execute at scale.

The Team

Corporate Development & Ventures at MongoDB is a small team focused on high-impact work with a bias toward action.

Our ventures platform maintains active coverage of the developer and AI ecosystem, providing consistent exposure to emerging companies, founders, and technologies.

In this role, you will be expected to translate that visibility into well-defined M&A and investment opportunities, bringing the judgment and execution needed to move from insight to completed transactions.

We’re looking for someone who

  • Has 8–12+ years of experience in Corporate Development, Investment Banking, Private Equity, or Venture Capital
  • Has led multiple M&A transactions end-to-end and can do so with limited oversight
  • Is comfortable being the most experienced and accountable deal operator in the room
  • Demonstrates consistently strong judgment, particularly under time pressure and incomplete information
  • Holds a high bar for analytical rigor, but knows when to make decisions without perfect data
  • Can engage credibly with technical leaders while maintaining a clear business perspective
  • Is direct, low-ego, and accountable—focused on outcomes, not process
  • Is motivated by building and operating at a high-performing standard, not maintaining one

What you will do

Lead Transactions That Matter

  • Own M&A and investment processes end-to-end, from initial engagement through close
  • Build and pressure-test valuation, structure, and strategic rationale independently
  • Run tight, focused diligence processes across technical, product, commercial, and financial dimensions
  • Drive decisions forward—without creating unnecessary process or delay

Set the Standard for Execution

  • Bring structure and clarity to complex, ambiguous situations
  • Identify what matters quickly and focus the team accordingly
  • Ensure transactions are well-understood, well-structur…

jobs.title: Director, Employee Communications
jobs.location: Austin; New York City; San Francisco
jobs.body:
We are hiring a Director of Employee Communications to reset and lead this function at a higher strategic and operational level. As we set out to become a $10B company in the age of AI, we must drive a high-performance culture grounded in clear strategy and operating at pace. To do this, we must raise the bar on how our internal comms operates and delivers the information employees need in order to execute successfully, while strengthening leadership alignment, employee trust and clarity during periods of change. This requires a Director-level leader who can set the strategy and operating model for the function, influence senior stakeholders, lead through others and drive into execution when needed with core partners, including executive leadership, the People team and Global Comms. It is important to base this role in the U.S. (San Francisco, NYC, or Austin) so the Director is in the time zones where leadership is primarily making decisions that directly impact employees and the internal communications function day-to-day in addition to supporting high-velocity, high-stakes communications with senior leaders in real-time. Early objectives include:

  • Reset the Program: The Director will define and implement our internal comms plan, operating cadence, channel strategy and quality bar to be consistent and proactive while delivering operational excellence at pace.
  • Lead cross-functional alignment with Global Comms and HR: They will closely collaborate with the People Team and will ensure internal communications are planned, aligned, and integrated into the company narrative, major business moments and leadership priorities.
  • Lead High-Stakes Moments: Own our most visible internal platforms, like Global MongoDB Lives. We need a leader on the ground to ensure these moments deliver. This role will oversee the execution of several of leaderships’ tentpole programs that shape company strategy, trust and organizational clarity.

MongoDB is building the data platform for the next generation of production-grade AI applications. We’re looking for a Director of Employee Communications to lead our internal communications strategy and execution. Reporting to the Sr. Director of Global Communications, you will own the internal communications function to keep our global employees aligned with our company strategy and engaged in our customer-obsessed mission, with a focus on clarity, trust and execution across the business.

We need a strategic operator who thrives on the ‘how’ and is energized by the challenge of giving our employees the clarity they need to succeed. You will set the direction for and run the day-to-day work of the internal communications function, with a focus on connecting our teams to our brand story, strategy and business outcomes while ensuring operational excellence across channels, company moments and leadership communications: you should be as comfortable refining a sensitive announcement a…

jobs.title: Director, Engineering
jobs.body:
MongoDB is seeking a Director of Engineering to join the Atlas Clusters Organization. The organization is responsible for building MongoDB Atlas, our database as a service offering and fastest growing product. Atlas allows users to deploy fault-tolerant, secure, globally distributed MongoDB clusters in just minutes. This includes developing software to interface with the three major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, and GCP) in order to bring security, durability, availability, and performance to all deployments of MongoDB.

As we continue to grow our Atlas Clusters teams in Dublin, we are looking for a Director of Engineering to lead teams that own critical pieces of both the Atlas Clusters Control Plane and Data Plane. 

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.

What you’ll do

  • Lead teams of motivated leads and individual contributors who are eager to learn and grow
  • Work with stakeholders throughout MongoDB to build our roadmap and product offerings
  • Collaborate with product management and technical senior leadership to design, develop, and ship improvements to the Atlas Clusters Control Plane and Data Plane
  • Ensure smooth and timely execution of projects while collaborating and providing visibility to all stakeholders
  • Work with customers during escalations and become part of our on-call rotation 
  • Grow your organization while ensuring desirable skill composition, high job satisfaction, and vibrant culture
  • Foster an inclusive and respectful work environment according to MongoDB's Core Values

We’re looking for someone who

  • Has at least 5 years of people management experience
  • Has at least 8 years of professional software development experience
  • Has led engineering teams that have built and maintained large scale systems
  • Extensive experience collaborating across organization boundaries
  • Proven success in designing, writing, testing, and maintaining large-scale, distributed backend systems
  • Has experience with at least one major cloud provider technology (AWS, Azure, GCP)
  • Is eager to solve tough problems
  • Has excellent communication skills
  • Is curious, collaborative, and motivated

Success Measures

  • In 1 month, you'll be familiar with the foundational parts of the system, the existing features, and projects within the Atlas Clusters team
  • In 3 months, you will be contributing to the delivery of your team’s projects and working on your team’s growth
  • In 6 months, you will be fully leading your team towards its product and engineering goals

About MongoDB

MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create…

jobs.title: Director, Global Quote-to-Cash Operations (India COE)
jobs.body:
MongoDB is growing and gaining adoption rapidly across the globe with the most demanding and innovative companies and development teams in enterprise software. 

We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Operations Finance Leader to join our team in India. This newly created position will be responsible for leading and developing a high-performing team to deliver world-class services expected from a Centre of Excellence operation. You will also work closely with the broader FinOps India leadership team to drive culture and connectedness and be an active member of the global finance leadership team. 

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Gurugram for our hybrid working model.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and develop a high-performing team of cross functional professionals to deliver world-class services to internal customers
  • Provide oversight for all finance and accounting operations and ensure timely delivery of services including relevant metrics and SLAs
  • Drive process improvements, standardization, globalization and operational efficiencies that is expected from a Centre of Excellence operation
  • Work collaboratively with other leaders in MongoDB to ensure alignment to functional strategy and consistency in service of our center of excellence provides to teams globally
  • Foster a culture of continuous improvement and a customer-centric mindset within the shared services team
  • Identify and mitigate operational and internal control risks Lead change management initiatives to drive adoption of new processes and technologies
  • Establish and maintain relationships with key stakeholders, including internal customers, senior leaders, and service providers
  • Develop and monitor performance metrics to track progress and ensure accountability
  • Act as business partner to the Senior Accounting Leadership and support in carrying out the organization priorities and objectives
  • Model ‘Growth Mindset’ at all times - show that improvement is always possible

Key Competencies Required

  • Outstanding communication skills in English (both oral and written)
  • Strategic, analytical and critical thinking skills
  • Personal commitment to accuracy, excellence and quality
  • Values driven and role model for the team
  • Dynamic self-starter that demonstrates creativity and initiative
  • Intrinsically motivated to learn and grow
  • Honesty and integrity

Requirements

  • A Qualified Chartered Accountant, MBA in Finance or any similar qualification preferred
  • 15+ years of experience in shared services operations, with at least 10 years of leadership experience
  • Strong understanding of shared services operations, including process improvement, standardization, and operational efficiencies
  • Experience in change management and driving adoptio…

jobs.title: Director, Identity & Security Product Management
jobs.location: Alberta; British Columbia; Manitoba; Nova Scotia; Ontario; Quebec; Toronto
jobs.body:
We are seeking a dynamic and visionary Director of Identity and Security Product Management to lead our Identity and Security Product Management team. This person will define and drive the execution of our product strategy for crucial functions across IAM and security, with a portfolio spanning authorization, authentication, Queryable Encryption, security hardening, and more. In this pivotal role, you will collaborate closely with stakeholders at every level of the organization, including executive leadership and cross-functional teams across multiple business units.

As the Director of Identity and Security Product Management, you will work closely with your engineering counterparts to define and deliver the roadmap for Identity and Access Management (IAM), Queryable Encryption, and Database Security. Your scope will span the MongoDB Atlas Platform, Core Database, and other critical companion services, ensuring that our IAM and security offerings continue to set the industry standard for excellence and innovation.

Your leadership in this role will be instrumental in maintaining our customers’ trust in MongoDB to deploy secure, high-performance applications with confidence, knowing their data is protected by cutting-edge security solutions.

This role can be based in Toronto or remotely in Canada.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the Identity & Security Product Management team responsible for delivering Identity and Access Management, Queryable Encryption, and other customer-facing security features within the MongoDB platform 
  • Drive highly regulated customer platform security and compliance requirements into MongoDB products
  • Drive collaboration across Engineering, Customer Support, Marketing, Sales, and the Executive Leadership Team
  • Drive a strategic narrative with customers and industry thought leaders around database security and identity
  • Develop and maintain an accurate and up-to-date roadmap across your ownership areas in partnership with Engineering counterparts
  • Develop strategic, operational, and tactical recommendations tailored to our products to improve  customers’ security posture and compliance positioning; regularly evangelize solutions at industry conferences, trade shows, and with key partners and accounts
  • Deeply understand all aspects of MongoDB - to ensure a business outcome aligned with our broader product, customer, and company priorities
  • Define security go-to-market strategies, identify key market/competitive trends and customer opportunities, and plan and manage programs to execute against those strategies by partnering with sales leadership at regional and global levels
  • Drive decision-making around prioritization, solutions, functionality, and pricing

Requirements

  • 7+ years of experience managing teams and demonstrating leadership skills that bring together diverse viewpoi…

jobs.title: Director, International Financial Compliance
jobs.body:
The International Entity Controller is responsible for the framework governing the Company’s legal entity accounting, statutory reporting, and regulatory compliance across all international jurisdictions. This leader ensures the Company’s entity structure operates within a disciplined control environment while delivering predictable statutory compliance, scalable reporting processes, and operational transparency across the legal entity structure.

The role also drives modernization of the statutory reporting and legal entity governance model, leveraging automation, standardized processes, and operational metrics to enable a scalable global controllership infrastructure.  The role reports into the Vice President, Corporate Controller.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.

Key Responsibilities

International  Entity Governance

  • Own the Company’s international legal entity governance framework, ensuring disciplined oversight of all international entities
  • Maintain comprehensive entity compliance calendars, statutory governance requirements, and local regulatory obligations across jurisdictions
  • Partner with Legal and Tax to support entity formation, restructuring, liquidations, and entity rationalisation initiatives
  • Ensure the legal entity structure remains operationally efficient, compliant, and aligned with the Company’s global business model
  • Partner with the Tax organization to support any applicable income and/or indirect (VAT) tax audits

Statutory Financial Reporting

  • Oversee preparation and filing of statutory financial statements and regulatory filings across all international jurisdictions
  • Ensure appropriate alignment between U.S. GAAP reporting and local statutory reporting frameworks (IFRS and local GAAP where required)
  • Maintain strong oversight of local statutory audit processes, ensuring consistent quality and predictable completion timelines

Intercompany & Cross-Border Accounting

  • Partner with the Corporate Accounting / General Ledger team to drive the global framework for intercompany accounting, settlements, and cross-border funding activities
  • Partner with Tax to operationalize transfer pricing policies and documentation requirements
  • Oversee accounting for capital contributions, dividends, intercompany loans, and other cross-border financing and treasury initiatives

Global Statutory Audit Management

  • Coordinate statutory audits globally across all jurisdictions
  • Drive standardized audit readiness processes, documentation standards, and audit coordination practices
  • Maintain strong relationships with local auditors and external service providers supporting statutory reporting

Legal Entity Structure Optimization

  • Evaluate and optimize the Co…

jobs.title: Director of Engineering, Query Optimization
jobs.body:
As the Director of Engineering for the MongoDB Query Optimization team, you will be responsible for leading the development of a world-class, open-source, general-purpose query engine designed for the document model. The distributed architecture of MongoDB and flexible schema open up an uncharted realm of captivating questions about query languages, cost-based optimization, schema inference, data federation, and many more. The Query Optimization team plays a crucial role in driving the vision, experience, and architecture of data processing for the entire organization.

As a director-level manager, you will directly manage a team of engineering managers and staff-level engineers. Although this is a people-management role, you must be able to zoom in to weigh-in on deeply technical decisions in the query processing domain.

Our global team is continuously expanding its charter and attracting the best query engineers working remotely or from hubs such as New York City, Dublin, Seattle, and Palo Alto. You will lead and grow a high-performing, diverse team and run large, cutting-edge initiatives. We are on a mission to write a new chapter in data processing and forever change how users think about supporting a variety of their workloads. We need your help to lead this journey!

​​This role can be based out of our Dublin office or remotely in the Republic of Ireland. Relocation assistance is provided.

Candidate Profile

  • At least 5 years of experience in a management position, including experience mentoring other engineering managers
  • Proven success building, mentoring, and growing strong geographically-distributed software engineering teams
  • High emotional intelligence and a commitment to building a culturally diverse and inclusive organization
  • Excellent verbal and written technical communication skills
  • Extensive experience collaborating across organization boundaries
  • Excellent time management skills and the ability to make complex project decisions quickly
  • Proven success in designing, writing, testing, and maintaining distributed and/or highly concurrent software systems in large, long-lived code bases. C/C++ knowledge is a plus
  • At least 10 years of experience in programming and system design with a strong grip on the foundational material of computer science
  • Professional or advanced academic expertise in the domains of query planning, optimization, and execution along with a deep understanding of systems programming, many-core architectures, and distributed data processing

Position Expectations

You will:

  • Collaborate with Product Management and technical senior leadership to design, develop, and ship a groundbreaking, multi-model, distributed query engine
  • Ensure smooth and timely execution of projects while collabo…

jobs.title: Director of Product Management, Developer Experience, Tools and Client Libraries
jobs.body:
We are looking for an outstanding and visionary product leader who is passionate about serving the developer community. This person will define and drive the execution of our product strategy and roadmap across the developer journey with a portfolio that spans MongoDB's traditional developer tools, MongoDB Compass, mongosh (MongoDB’s shell), Atlas CLI, IDE integrations and extensions and local development experiences, to libraries such as language drivers and application frameworks, to MongoDB’s offerings to support those on the forefront of developing with AI–such as our MCP server and AI framework support. You will collaborate closely with stakeholders at every level of the organization, including executive leadership and cross-functional teams across multiple business units, to ensure that all MongoDB features are seamlessly woven into one of the best developer experiences in technology as we move into the age of AI.   

Your leadership in this role will be instrumental in not just maintaining, but increasing and intensifying developers’ love of MongoDB.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.

What You'll Do

  • Lead and develop a high-performing product team. You'll manage a team of product managers, including managers of managers, creating an environment where people grow, stay challenged, and do their best work. You'll be a hands-on coach who can help your team get unstuck — not just a strategist, but someone who has shipped complex products themselves
  • Define and own a coherent vision across a broad portfolio. Developer Experience isn't a single product — it's a collection of interconnected tools, libraries, and frameworks. You'll develop a strategy that makes the whole greater than the sum of its parts, and communicate that vision clearly to stakeholders up and down the organization
  • Set goals, track the right metrics, and drive outcomes. Developer tooling doesn't have a direct revenue line, but it has a profound impact on activation, retention, and churn. You'll build a strong partnership with product analytics to identify the right signals, formulate meaningful metrics, and use data to drive decisions
  • Be a credible technical voice. You'll engage deeply with engineering directors and architects on decisions involving network protocols, distributed systems, client libraries, and simplification of our driver stack. You won't write the code, but you'll need to understand it well enough to help the team make the right tradeoffs, communicate confidently with technical customers, and be a strong advocate for the developers out there who are building on MongoDB
  • Champion AI as a first-class capability. Developers are both increasingly relying on AI to build but also build AI-native applications, and MongoDB is at the center of this shift. You'll help ensure our developer tooling is purpose-built for the AI era —…

jobs.title: Director, Product Marketing
jobs.body:
As a Director of Product Marketing, you will play a pivotal role in helping our teams achieve outcomes tied to the organization's most complex and strategic work. This is your chance to define and deliver outcomes with significant impact, including being directly involved in driving the strategy and execution for a portfolio of high-impact initiatives.

Reporting to the Senior Director, Head of Product Marketing, you will work with leaders across Product, Engineering, Sales, and Marketing to build go-to-market (GTM) strategies and successfully lead cross-functional initiatives. You will act as a key partner to senior leadership and the executive team to influence and drive complex decisions and evaluate trade-offs across the company.

The ideal candidate is based in our Bay Area or New York City hub locations. Flexible or remote options within the United States will be considered for the right candidate.

What You'll Do

  • Define Strategy: Own and define the end-to-end product marketing strategy for an established product portfolio and new, high-growth business areas, including top-level platform narratives
  • Lead High-Impact GTM: Develop strong, data-driven opinions and provide clear guidance to your team and the broader organization on all aspects of GTM strategy, including positioning, messaging, personas (user and buyer), sales enablement, and launch execution
  • Solve the Hardest Problems: Identify, prioritize, and decompose intrinsically hard problems. You will proactively identify gaps and opportunities, pressure-test strategic assumptions, and create the correct sense of urgency to deliver results
  • Influence Executive Leadership: Act as a key partner to senior leadership. You will be expected to clearly present solutions to complex problems, help leaders understand their options, and drive alignment across business units
  • Build Scalable Mechanisms: Create, optimize, and drive the adoption of critical mechanisms and processes that ensure your programs are successful in the long term, enabling other teams to work more independently and effectively
  • Mentor and Amplify: Serve as a role model and bar raiser for the entire Product Marketing organization. You will amplify your impact by educating others on leadership and best practices while actively guiding the career growth of your direct and indirect reports

We’re Excited About You Because

  • Proven experience in product marketing, preferably in B2B technology, cloud, or enterprise software, with a strong track record of success
  • A demonstrated ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, where you are given a high-level business outcome to achieve, and trusted to develop the strategy from scratch
  • Proven ability to bring strong, data-driven business and strategic judgment to decisions, with a keen ability to assess risk and drive clarity
  • Consensus-building a…

jobs.title: Director, Regional Employee Experience
jobs.body:
The Director, Employee Experience India will report to the Head of Regional HR, APAC and will be the face of the People Team to the India employees and managers while serving as the main point of contact for escalated inquiries and concerns, performance management, employee well-being, employee relations, and compliance. Additionally, this role will proactively and collaboratively, support and partner a local business unit leader and their team, while overseeing and/or directly leading the local Employee Experience team. 

The Employee Experience Team at MongoDB India is the face of HR to the approximately 800 employees. The team is responsible for providing full employee life cycle service delivery from onboarding to separation management in partnership with the rest of the People Team including the following CoEs: Recruiting, Total Rewards, Learning and Development, Employee Engagement & Inclusion, HR Business Partnering and HR Operations. The team is also responsible for our Workplace sustainability and community/events efforts, Employee Relations, policy and process enhancements including regional compliance as well as developing programs to enhance manager capability across the company. 

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Gurugram for our hybrid working model.

Key Responsibilities

  • Management: Effectively lead India Employee Experience team and manage direct report(s), with effective cadence, fostering a collaborative and productive work environment while actively supporting their professional development 
  • Partnership: Partner with global HRBPs and COEs to support achieving the people goals of assigned business units in India
  • Service Delivery: Serve as the main point of contact for escalated employee and people manager questions and concerns for your assigned business unit and broader India businesses.
  • Performance Management: Coach and enable managers to set clear expectations, provide regular feedback, and manage employee performance
  • Employee Relations: Support our commitment to a safe and balanced workplace by evaluating employee complaints to make appropriate recommendations to address the matter. Conduct mediation sessions when necessary.
  • Process Improvement: Develop and evolve MongoDB People processes and systems to continue elevating the employee and manager experience
  • Compliance: Stay updated on employment law and current legislation related to Human Resources, providing valuable advice to managers, identifying risks, and suggesting alternative solutions. Contribute to the evaluation, development, and enhancement of company policies as needed
  • Culture Ambassador: Focus on manager and leadership enablement; support and promote a values-based culture and effective hybrid working environment in partnership with Workplace and local leadership
  • Coaching: Act as a trusted advisor, applying your HR…

jobs.title: Director, Strategic Sourcing
jobs.body:
MongoDB is seeking a highly motivated Director of Strategic Sourcing to lead a globally distributed team of Procurement professionals.  This role is responsible for driving complex strategic sourcing strategies across all spend categories in support of the company’s business strategies.  As a key member of the Source-to-Pay cross functional team, you will be called upon to fly at different altitudes to help executives and individual contributors make better purchasing decisions.

The ideal candidate is a self-starter who has the knowledge, skill, and discipline to transform the function into our next stage of growth.  You should have deep expertise in driving technology deals such as public cloud infrastructure agreements, SaaS, IT services, while still having working knowledge of Marketing, professional services, and other indirect categories.

This will be a hybrid in-office role. We are looking for candidates who plan to be available in our New York City office for approximately 2 days per week.

Key Responsibilities: What You’ll Be Doing 

  • Drive category strategy to all applicable spend categories for MongoDB
  • Develop and maintain global procurement policies and procedures
  • Develop, coach, and lead a high-performing global procurement team, while fostering a culture of continuous improvement, accountability, knowledge sharing, and innovation
  • Be the face of Procurement by developing and maintaining strong relationships with key leaders and stakeholders and reinforcing the strategic value that the team provides
  • Deliver annual cost savings target and multi-year pipeline based on assigned projects
  • Implement and lead process improvement projects along with any change management required

Qualifications: What We’ll Need to See 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Supply Chain, Finance, or similar quantitative related field
  • Procurement professional certification, such as CPSM, CIPS, CSP
  • 12+ overall years of professional business experience
  • 10+ years of progressive experience in Procurement and/or Strategic Sourcing
  • 3+ years of people leadership experience
  • Deep knowledge of strategic sourcing and negotiation
  • Hands-on experience in negotiating complex deals
  • Leader with vision and credentials working at global large scale companies
  • Strong interpersonal and leadership skills
  • Ability to drive change and influence all levels of the organization
  • Experience analyzing large data sets while developing and driving recommendations from collected insights

How To Stand Out From Other Candidates 

  • MBA or equivalent experience
  • Working knowledge of Source to Pay Orchestration tools
  • Experience with large ERP systems such as NetSuite, Oracle, SAP, or Workday
  • Experience using Coupa (including Co…

jobs.title: Director, Tax
jobs.body:
The International Tax Director will execute all aspects of international tax including compliance, transfer pricing, and planning. The International Tax Director will manage a team of tax professionals, collaborate with external advisors, and work closely with finance, legal, and business units to support global tax initiatives.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Austin, TX for our hybrid working model.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Support IP and tax structure planning to maximize tax efficiency 
  • Direct global tax compliance 
  • Direct international quarterly and year-end accounting for income taxes (ASC 740).  Direct international tax provisions for foreign statutory financials
  • Execute the Company's global transfer pricing strategy and policy including monitoring controlled transactions, transfer prices and adjustments
  • Prepare and maintain intercompany agreements and allocations
  • Review the tax components of certain customer agreements
  • Review business initiatives to determine the tax impact  
  • Direct corporate international tax research and interpretation
  • Manage international tax audits 
  • Direct post-acquisition integrations
  • Work closely with Treasury in managing cash globally
  • Coordinate with outside tax consultants and contractors for various tax projects 

Qualifications:

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (CPA, MST or LLM (Taxation) preferred)

Experience:

  • 10+ years of progressive experience in corporate tax, with at least 5 years in a leadership role
  • Strong compliance and planning experience in a multi-national, multi-entity environment including the areas of: BEAT, GILTI, FDII, Subpart F, OECD Pillar Two, Check the Box Principles, U.S. Tax Treaty Research and Interpretation, Intellectual and Intangible Property Planning, and Transfer Pricing
  • Working knowledge of international taxation in Europe and Asia

Skills:

  • Understanding of SEC and GAAP reporting requirements
  • Significant project management skills required
  • Ability to navigate complex tax issues and provide strategic solutions
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities

About MongoDB

MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is…

jobs.title: Engagement Manager (Services Sales)
jobs.body:
MongoDB Engagement Managers are quota-carrying Professional Services Sales roles. Engagement managers utilize customer-facing sales, technical, consulting, and commercial experience to scope, negotiate and close Professional Services opportunities to accelerate and de-risk the adoption of MongoDB by our customers.

As an Engagement Manager, you will be a key leader within the PS team and work cross-functionally with the Sales, Professional Services, and Customer Success organizations to drive professional services sales.

Here are a few informative blogs about the Engagement Management role:

https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/engagement-management-mongodb-meet-lalitesh-pal

https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/how-engagement-managers-help-customers-succeed

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in San Francisco, CA for our hybrid working model.

What you’ll do at MongoDB

  • Understand customers’ overall portfolio of applications and datasets, IT and business priorities and success measures to adapt, develop and design specific digital transformation approaches involving MongoDB technologies
  • Help transform companies by translating their use-cases, pains, and needs into scoped projects and statements of work
  • Partner with a best-in-class sales organization and work effectively as a part of a larger team to develop account strategies and plans for the successful adoption, growth, and utilization of MongoDB
  • Have a solid sense of ownership, driving all deals or technical scopings from inception to closure
  • Manage the Professional Services pipeline for bookings and revenue forecasts for your region
  • Expertly articulate the business value of professional services, talking knowledgeably and credibly about service delivery issues, challenges, strategies, approaches, and mitigating risks
  • Work with our practice team to propagate internal skills and experience through the development of repeatable assets (estimates, proposals, SOWs, templates, tools, case studies etc.)
  • Work with the delivery team to transition any agreed upon project scope and customer expectations to ensure successful project delivery
  • Proactively seek opportunities to support and mentor other team members and share best practices
  • Have situational awareness and react appropriately in group settings

An Ideal candidate will have

  • A BS degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering or equivalent professional experience
  • A minimum of 5 years experience in the sale of Professional Services and/or technology solutions at an enterprise software company
  • Experience as an architect, consultant…

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Search Query Platform & Availability
jobs.body:
MongoDB Atlas Search and Vector Search let developers run complex search queries and build AI-powered applications directly inside MongoDB, without managing a separate search infrastructure. It is a product used by a large and growing number of customers who rely on it being fast, reliable, and available.

The Search Query Platform & Availability (SQPA) team is a new team being built from the ground up. Its purpose is to own the foundational layer that keeps Atlas Search stable and scalable, and to give the engineers building search features the tools they need to move faster and ship with more confidence. The team's work spans three areas: keeping search reliable under pressure through resource controls and safeguards; building the tooling that helps Search engineers roll out changes safely at scale; and maintaining the engine, storage and dependencies that everything else depends on.

You will be the first leader of this team. That means you are not stepping into an existing structure. You are shaping the roadmap, building the team, and establishing how SQPA operates within a broader Search org that is growing quickly.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Toronto, Ontario Canada for our hybrid working model.

What you'll do

  • Lead and grow a team of software engineers, owning all aspects of people management including hiring, performance, career development, and compensation
  • Define and drive the 12-month roadmap for SQPA in close collaboration with stakeholders across Search, Platform, and Product
  • Improve the processes and tooling that allow the Search org to make changes confidently in high-stakes parts of the codebase
  • Own and lead resolution of time-sensitive customer escalations related to search availability and performance
  • Partner with peer engineering leads to establish clear ownership boundaries and make sure platform improvements create real value for the teams relying on them
  • Collaborate with product managers, program managers, and engineering teams across the company on large cross-functional initiatives
  • Contribute to the long-term vision for Search at MongoDB and help develop a plan to get there

Who we're looking for

  • 2+ years of experience managing a team of software engineers, including hiring, coaching, and performance conversations
  • A technical background in building or operating complex software systems, such as databases, distributed systems, query engines or data infrastructure at scale
  • Prior experience with search systems or search infrastructure is a plus, but not required
  • Strong judgment about when to go deep, when to delegate, and when to push back on scope or priority
  • Comfort operating with some ambiguity. This team is new and the roadmap is still being shaped. The right person sees that as an opportunity to have real influence, not a reason to…

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Server Networking Security (Rust)
jobs.body:
Come join and lead the Server Ingress Security team, where we are rearchitecting MongoDB Server’s ingress networking to make MongoDB clusters even more secure. This new team is building the Atlas Network Protection layer, a set of performant, security-critical services that harden MongoDB's pre-authentication attack surface and provides the ability to respond rapidly to emergent threats. We are looking for a talented Lead Engineer to join the team and be founding members, where you will play a crucial role in our multi-year roadmap.

Our team champions a strong culture of inclusivity, diversity, and collaboration, and lives MongoDB cultural values every day – we value intellectual curiosity and honesty, and building together in an environment that prioritizes collaboration over competition. If you want to lead a fast-growing team that applies security and systems engineering fundamentals to protect a popular database at scale, join us!

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.

Candidate Profile

  • 3+ years of experience managing a team of software engineers, including hiring, performance and growth management, compensation planning, and mentoring
  • You have 8+ years of experience building production-quality systems software with large backend/compiled codebases, ideally in Rust.
    • Bonus points for experience with performance profiling, network protocols, TLS, and connection management
  • You have strong technical judgment that you use to effectively guide engineering decisions in security-sensitive or networking-adjacent domains
    • You put the customer first and don't hesitate to cross team boundaries in search of the right solution
  • Solid experience in designing, writing, testing, maintaining, and operating mission-critical software systems
    • Bonus points Professional or advanced academic expertise in the domains of security or networking
  • You enjoy coaching, career development, and creating growth opportunities to help your team reach their full potential
  • You care about building a diverse, inclusive environment where engineers can do their best work
  • Regardless of prior experience, you are willing, able, and excited to quickly learn new things in the domains of computer science, software engineering, and leadership

Position Expectations

  • Lead a team of engineers, managing all aspects of people management including hiring, growth, compensation, and performance management
  • Be directly responsible for the results your team delivers
  • Lead the design, implementation, and operation production-ready security services in Rust, with a focus on security, correctness, performance, and operational excellence
  • Own the definition and execution of the team's 12-month roadmap for the Atlas Network Protection layer
  • Work…

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
jobs.body:
MongoDB’s mission is to empower innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries by unleashing the power of software and data. We enable organizations of all sizes to easily build, scale, and run modern applications by helping them modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our industry-leading developer data platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available in more than 115 regions across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. Atlas allows customers to build and run applications anywhere—on premises, or across cloud providers. With offices worldwide and over 175,000 new developers signing up to use MongoDB every month, it’s no wonder that leading organizations, like Samsung and Toyota, trust MongoDB to build next-generation, AI-powered applications.

About the job

The worldwide data management software market is massive (According to IDC, the worldwide database software market, which it refers to as the database management systems software market, was forecasted to be approximately $82 billion in 2023 growing to approximately $137 billion in 2027. This represents a 14% compound annual growth rate). At MongoDB we are transforming industries and empowering developers to build amazing apps that people use every day. We are the leading developer data platform and the first database provider to IPO in over 20 years. Join our team and be at the forefront of innovation and creativity.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dubai for our hybrid working model.

About the Sales Culture

MongoDB is always developing and innovating — not only in our technology, but also in our sales go-to-market strategy. Our sales leadership is committed to building the best salesforce in technology. This means, inspiring and enabling success for everyone on the team. We not only equip you to be successful and close deals, but we want your feedback and input on how we can continue to “Think Big and Go Far.” As a crucial part of the Sales team at MongoDB, you will have access to a lucrative market and learn how to sell from some of the most successful sales leaders in the software industry.

What you will be doing

  • Proactively, identify, qualify and close a sales pipeline

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
jobs.body:
Get to Know the Sales Team

Our sales team’s success is MongoDB’s success. We help customers solve challenges and build their next big thing. And our mission is to bring our developer data platform to new markets and new businesses around the globe. Our sales leadership is committed to building the best salesforce in technology. This means, inspiring and enabling success for everyone on the team. We not only equip you to be successful, but we want your feedback and input on how we can continue to “Think Big and Go Far.” 

The Opportunity

As a Senior Enterprise Account Executive, Growth you will play a pivotal role in driving the growth and success of MongoDB. You will be responsible for identifying and pursuing new business opportunities within an existing customer base, cultivating relationships with key stakeholders, and ensuring the successful adoption of our solutions.

What You’ll Be Doing

Prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT leaders, and technical end usersGrow a current base of MongoDB customers by landing new workloads through outbound prospectingManage the full sales cycle for all MongoDB Products & ServicesDevelop and execute a strategic territory plan to achieve revenue targetsCollaborate with the sales ecosystem to drive sales initiatives and partnershipsBuild a sales pipeline in Salesforce and Clari while maximizing prospecting tools such as Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, Sendoso, and Outreach

What You’ll Bring To The Role

5+ years of quota-carrying field sales experience with a focus on closing new workloads and expanding accounts. Demonstrated ability to successfully expand existing accounts and sell horizontally across the organization into different business units.A proven track record of overachievement and hitting sales targetsAbility to articulate the business value of complex enterprise technologyCo-selling with cloud service providers (AWS, GCP and Azure) and large GSIs (Accenture, KPMG, etc) is a plusMust live in territory (flexible work model)Fluent in English 

Things We Love

Passionate about growing your career in the largest market in software (database)Previous Sales Methodology training (e.g. MEDDIC, SPIN, Challenger Sales)Familiarity with databases, develops and open source technology a plus

Why Join MongoDB

  • MongoDB invests above the industry average in development of each of our new hires & continuous career development
  • Accelerators up to 30%
  • Comprehensive Sales trainings in MEDDIC and Command of the Message, including our Sales Bootcamps and development programs
  • New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan
  • Generous and competitive benefits (parental leave, fertility & wellbeing support)

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Acquisition ( Indonesia - Bahasa Speaker Required)
jobs.body:
About the Role

We’re looking for a hardworking, driven individual with superb energy, passion, and initiative for growing business within strategic enterprise accounts in the Indonesia and wider Bahasa-speaking markets.

The Enterprise Account Executive, Growth role focuses exclusively on formulating and executing a sales strategy within an assigned territory, resulting in revenue growth and new customer acquisition. You will work closely with our broader ASEAN team to deepen our footprint with key customers and prospects across Indonesia and other Bahasa-speaking segments, driving strategic, high-impact deals.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Singapore for our hybrid working model (3 days per week in office).

MongoDB’s Sales Culture

MongoDB is always developing and innovating — not only in our technology, but also in our sales go-to-market strategy. Our sales leadership is committed to building the best salesforce in technology, inspiring and enabling success for everyone on the team.

As a crucial part of the Sales team at MongoDB, you will have access to a lucrative market and learn how to sell from some of the most successful sales leaders in the software industry.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Proactively identify, qualify, and close a healthy, predictable sales pipeline across Indonesia and Bahasa-speaking markets within ASEAN
  • Strategically prospect into CTOs, CIOs, Engineering/IT leaders, and technical end users, tailoring your value proposition to the needs of Bahasa-speaking stakeholders
  • Build strong and effective relationships with executive sponsors and operational teams, resulting in multi-year growth opportunities and expanded footprint
  • Partner with our Solution Architects and work closely with Professional Services and Customer Success to drive successful adoption and long-term value for customers
  • Collaborate with our enterprise ecosystem partner sales and channel partners in Indonesia and ASEAN to maximize deal sizes and expand MongoDB’s presence in the region
  • Accurately forecast and manage your territory, ensuring timely and transparent communication of risks and opportunities
  • Participate in our sales enablement trainings, including our comprehensive Sales Bootcamp, sophisticated sales training, and leadership and development programs

What You Bring to the Table

  • 8+ years of field, quota-carrying experience in a fast-paced and competitive market, with a focus on expanding new and existing enterprise accounts
  • Demonstrated ability to run complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles and sell into new lines of business within open accounts
  • A proven track record of consistent overachievement and hitting or exceeding sales targets
  • Ability to articulate the business value of complex enterprise technology to both technical and non-technical audiences

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Growth
jobs.body:
MongoDB’s mission is to empower innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries by unleashing the power of software and data. We enable organizations of all sizes to easily build, scale, and run modern applications by helping them modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our industry-leading developer data platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available in more than 115 regions across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. Atlas allows customers to build and run applications anywhere—on premises, or across cloud providers. With offices worldwide and over 175,000 new developers signing up to use MongoDB every month, it’s no wonder that leading organizations, like Samsung and Toyota, trust MongoDB to build next-generation, AI-powered applications.

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Growth
jobs.body:
About the role 

We’re looking for a hardworking, driven individual with superb energy, passion and initiative for growing and managing business. The Enterprise Account Executive role focuses exclusively on formulating and executing a sales strategy within the Financial Services Business in Eastern Europe. We’re looking to speak with candidates based in Poland, and who can be in the office 1 or 2 days a week. 

This role will be based remotely in Poland.

Position Expectations/Role Responsibilities/What you’ll do...

  • Proactively, identify, qualify and close a sales pipeline
  • Strategically prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT Leaders, & technical end users
  • Build strong and effective relationships, resulting in growth opportunities
  • Partner with our Solution Architects and work closely with the Professional Services team to achieve customer satisfaction
  • Work closely with the enterprise ecosystem partner sales and channel partner to maximize deal sizes
  • Participate in our sales enablement trainings, including our comprehensive Sales Bootcamp, sophisticated sales training, and leadership and development programs

Qualifications/Requirements/We’re looking for someone with…

  • Experience working enterprise and large Financial Services, Banks and Insurances accounts, opening them from scratch
  • Strategic mindset, experience selling to C-Level, focusing on key account initiatives
  • Examples of winning strategic deals that are core to the customers business
  • Multiple examples of 6 figure deals, and having grown a single account to at least $1M ARR Consistent over achievement against quota with a proven process in place to do this
  • At least 4 years experience selling to Eastern European customers face to face. MEDDPICC / Command of the message sales training is a bonus
  • Experience selling a technical product, with a complex sales cycle
  • Technical degree in Computer Science of Engineering is a bonus

About MongoDB

MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.
 
With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.
 
Our compass at MongoDB is…

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Growth
jobs.body:
Get to Know the Sales Team

Our sales team’s success is MongoDB’s success. We help customers solve challenges and build their next big thing. And our mission is to bring our developer data platform to new markets and new businesses around the globe. Our sales leadership is committed to building the best salesforce in technology. This means, inspiring and enabling success for everyone on the team. We not only equip you to be successful, but we want your feedback and input on how we can continue to “Think Big and Go Far.” 

The Opportunity

As an Enterprise Account Executive, Growth, you will play a pivotal role in driving the growth and success of MongoDB. You will be responsible for identifying and pursuing new business opportunities within an existing customer base, cultivating relationships with key stakeholders, and ensuring the successful adoption of our solutions.

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT leaders, and technical end users
  • Grow a current base of MongoDB customers by landing new workloads through outbound prospecting
  • Manage the full sales cycle for all MongoDB Products & Services
  • Develop and execute a strategic territory plan to achieve revenue targets
  • Collaborate with the sales ecosystem to drive sales initiatives and partnerships
  • Build a sales pipeline in Salesforce and Clari while maximizing prospecting tools such as Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, Sendoso, and Outreach

What You’ll Bring To The Role

  • 5+ years of quota-carrying field sales experience with a focus on closing new workloads and expanding accounts
  • Demonstrated ability to successfully expand existing accounts and sell horizontally across the organization into different business units
  • A proven track record of overachievement and hitting sales targets
  • Ability to articulate the business value of complex enterprise technology
  • Co-selling with cloud service providers (AWS, GCP and Azure) and large GSIs (Accenture, KPMG, etc) is a plus
  • Must live in territory (flexible work model)
  • Fluent in English 

Things We Love

  • Passionate about growing your career in the largest market in software (database)
  • Previous Sales Methodology training (e.g. MEDDIC, SPIN, Challenger Sales)
  • Familiarity with databases, develops and open source technology a plus

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Growth
jobs.body:
About the role

우리는 비즈니스를 성장시키고 관리하는 데 열정적이고 에너지가 넘치는 성실한 분을 찾고 있습니다. Enterprise Account Executive 역할은 맡은 고객 내에서 세일즈 전략을 수립하고 실행하여 매출 성장을 이루는 데 중점을 둡니다.

우리의 하이브리드 업무 방침에 따라 대한민국 서울 오피스로 출퇴근이 가능하신 분을 모시고자 합니다. (서울시 강남구 테헤란로 518)

What you will be doing

  • 적극적으로 세일즈 파이프라인을 찾고, 검증하며, 마감합니다.
  • CTO, 엔지니어링 및 IT 리더, 기술 최종 사용자에게 전략적으로 접근합니다.
  • 강력하고 효과적인 관계를 구축하여 성장 기회를 창출합니다.
  • 솔루션 아키텍트 및 프로페셔널 서비스 팀과 협력하여 고객 만족을 달성합니다.
  • 엔터프라이즈 생태계 파트너 영업 조직 및 채널 파트너와 긴밀히 협력하여 딜 규모를 극대화합니다.
  • 잘 짜여진 세일즈 부트캠프, 고급 세일즈 트레이닝 및 리더십 개발 프로그램을 포함한 세일즈 교육에 참여합니다.

What you bring to the table

  • 빠르게 변화하고 경쟁이 치열한 마켓에서 5년 이상의 다이렉트 세일즈 경험, 신규 고객 확보 및 고객 확대 경험, 새로운 고객을 확보하고 복잡한 영업 프로세스를 운영해본 경험
  • 영업 목표 초과 달성 경험 (President’s Club, Rep of the Year 등)
  • 복잡한 엔터프라이즈급 소프트웨어의 비즈니스 가치를 명확히 설명할 수 있는 능력
  • 비즈니스 챔피언을 구축할 수 있는 역량
  • 경쟁적이고 성공에 대한 강한 열망을 가진 분
  • 우수한 영어 활용 능력

Things we love

  • 최대 소프트웨어 시장(데이터베이스)에서 경력을 개발하고자 하는 열정
  • 세일즈 방법론 교육 이수 혹은 이를 활용한 실무 경험 (예: MEDDIC, SPIN, Challenger Sale)
  • 데이터베이스, 소프트웨어 개발 및 오픈 소스 기술에 대한 친숙함

Why join now

  • 새로운 직원의 역량 개발 및 지속적인 경력 개발에 대한 전폭적인 지원
  • 최대 25%의 커미션 엑셀러레이터
  • MEDDIC 및 Command of the Message를 포함한 최고 수준의 세일즈 교육, 포괄적인 세일즈 부트캠프 및 개발 프로그램
  • 신규 입사자 RSU (Restricted Stock Units) 및 ESPP (Employee Stock Purchase Plan)
  • 관대하고 경쟁력 있는 복지 혜택 (10% 퇴직연금 적립, 최대 20주의 유급 출산 휴가, 가족 단체보험, 불임 치료 및 웰빙 지원)

MongoDB’s Sales Culture

MongoDB는 기술뿐만 아니라 세일즈 시장 진출 전략에서도 항상 발전하고 혁신하고 있습니다. 우리의 세일즈 리더십은 최고의 세일즈 팀을 구축하는 데 전념하고 있으며, 팀의 모든 구성원이 성공할 수 있도록 영감을 주고 지원합니다. 우리는 여러분이 성공적으로 딜을 성사시킬 수 있도록 지원할 뿐만 아니라, "Think Big and Go Far"라는 우리의 목표를 계속 발전시킬 수 있도록 여러분의 피드백과 의견을 원합니다. MongoDB 세일즈 팀의 중요한 일원으로서, 여러분은 수익성 있는 시장에 다가갈 수 있으며, 소프트웨어 산업에서 가장 성공적인 세일즈 리더들로부터 영업 방법을 배울 수 있습니다.

About MongoDB

MongoDB는 변화를 위해 만들어졌습니다. 우리는 고객과 구성원이 시장의 속도에 맞춰 혁신할 수 있도록 힘을 실어줍니다. 우리는 AI 시대를 위한 데이터베이스를 새롭게 정의하여, 혁신가들이 소프트웨어로 산업을 창조하고, 변화시키고, 혁신할 수 있도록 합니다. MongoDB의 통합 데이터베이스 플랫폼은 시장에서 가장 널리 보급되고 전 세계적으로 분산된 데이터베이스입니다. 이 플랫폼은 조직이 기존 레거시 워크로드를 현대화하고, 혁신을 수용하며, AI를 마음껏 활용할 수 있도록 지원합니다. 클라우드 네이티브 플랫폼인 MongoDB Atlas는 AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure에서 모두 사용할 수 있는 유일한 전 세계 분산 멀티클라우드 데이터베이스입니다.

전 세계 오피스에서 일하고 있는 MongoDB는 약 60,000명의 고객을 보유하고 있으며, 여기에는 포춘 100대 기업의 75%와 AI 기반 스타트업이 포함됩니다. 이들은 가장 중요한 애플리케이션을 위해 MongoDB를 사용하고 있으며, 우리는 이들과 함께 소프트웨어의 새로운 시대를 열어가고 있습니다.

MongoDB에서 우리의 나침반은 Leadership Commitment입니다. 이는 우리가 어떻게, 그리고 왜 결정을 내리는지, 서로에게 어떻게 행동하는지, 그리고 어떻게 성공하는지를 안내합니다. 이것이 MongoDB를 MongoDB답게 만드는 요소입니다.

우리는 모든 구성원의 개인적 성장과 비즈니스 영향력을 높이기 위해, 모든 사람에게 지지와 풍요로움을 제공하는 문화를 만드는 데 헌신하고 있습니다.

jobs.body:
MongoDB’s mission is to empower innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries by unleashing the power of software and data. We enable organizations of all sizes to easily build, scale, and run modern applications by helping them modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our industry-leading developer data platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available in more than 115 regions across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. Atlas allows customers to build and run applications anywhere—on premises, or across cloud providers. With offices worldwide and over 175,000 new developers signing up to use MongoDB every month, it’s no wonder that leading organizations, like Samsung and Toyota, trust MongoDB to build next-generation, AI-powered applications.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in NYC for our hybrid working model.

Our sales team’s success is MongoDB’s success. We help customers solve challenges and build their next big thing. And our mission is to bring our developer data platform to new markets and new businesses around the globe. Our sales leadership is committed to building the best salesforce in technology. We enable organizations of all sizes to easily build, scale, and run modern applications by helping them modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our industry-leading developer data platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available in more than 115 regions across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. Atlas allows customers to build and run applications anywhere—on premises, or across cloud providers. With offices worldwide and over 175,000 new developers signing up to use MongoDB every month, it’s no wonder that leading organizations, like Samsung and Toyota, trust MongoDB to build next-generation, AI-powered applications.

jobs.title: Executive Business Partner
jobs.body:
The database market is enormous and accelerating. Industry analysts now estimate this market to exceed $150B in 2026, and MongoDB is at the head of its disruption. The MongoDB community is transforming industries and empowering developers to build amazing apps that people use every day. We are the leading modern data platform and the first database provider to IPO in over 20 years. Join our team and be at the forefront of innovation and creativity. 

MongoDB is seeking an Executive Business Partner to support our Chief Information Officer. The role will be based out of our Palo Alto office. 

This position is ideal for a dynamic, intelligent, highly-organized individual who takes ownership over the details and loves to get things done. In this role, your day will consist of managing and prioritizing a steady stream of requests, meetings, and trips, and working closely with the leaders and members of their teams. 

To succeed, you’ll have to balance the needs of multiple parties thoughtfully and diplomatically and be ready to pitch in wherever necessary. You should feel comfortable interacting with high-level executives and know how and when to manage in any direction in the organization, independent of role, title, or level. You can navigate Google Suite products or comparable ones and have proficiency to learn the necessary functions to execute tasks. You have excellent written skills and verbal skills. We have a team-based collaborative culture, and the successful candidate will enjoy building relationships and teamwork just as much as the challenges posed by the role.

Responsibilities

  • Manage multiple requests and priorities of the Leaders while staying calm under pressure, exercising judgment, and seeing beyond the day-to-day to anticipate changing needs
  • Manage the leader's calendar meticulously and thoughtfully, coordinating all meetings and calls, including video conferencing and team events
  • Manage all travel and expenses, ready to handle international itineraries and last-minute changes
  • Lead, drive, and execute various team events and offsite meetings, including ushering contracts through the procurement process and accounts payable for payment. At times, you will need to assist with onsite logistics of the events
  • Liaise with executives to drive, create, and review correspondence, spreadsheets, slide decks, and meeting agendas 
  • Track action items and key issues to closure with a high degree of integrity and accountability
  • Think and plan ahead, delegate responsibilities appropriately, and manage time effectively
  • Work with the senior leaders (direct reports, skip-levels, etc.) as needed to execute the duties of the job
  • Work cross-functionally with a diverse range of individuals at all levels in the organization
  • Assist with or deliver special assignments/projects
  • Occasionally…

jobs.title: Field CTO
jobs.body:
MongoDB is growing and gaining adoption rapidly across the globe with the most demanding and innovative companies and development teams in enterprise software. 

We are seeking an exceptional Principal Technologist to join our team. This pivotal role will be responsible for driving the adoption of MongoDB among our most strategic customers by engaging senior technical leadership to position MongoDB as the ideal solution for modernizing legacy applications and building modern, GenAI applications.

This role can be based out of remotely in the APAC region.

Key Responsibilities

  • Engage and influence executives and technical decision-makers at Fortune 1000 companies and create awareness and understanding of our products
  • Aligned with Sales, work with our Strategic Developer Relations team (comprised of senior engineers with deep MongoDB experience) to develop and deliver technical engagements and targeted education sessions
  • Develop and maintain deep technical expertise in MongoDB's product and solutions as well as industry trends and competitors' offerings
  • Gather feedback and assess customer sentiment to influence product roadmap and advocate for our customers
  • Represent MongoDB at industry conferences, trade shows, and technical events.
  • Contribute to thought leadership through blog posts, whitepapers, and speaking engagements.

Qualifications

  • 10+ years of technical consulting experience in Principal-level roles in Solutions Architecture, Developer Advocacy, Software Architecture, or Enterprise Consulting
  • Recognized technical leader with deep hands-on experience implementing enterprise-scale RDBMS, MongoDB, or other database solutions
  • Proven track record of enterprise project ownership and team leadership, preferably in modernization initiatives involving migration of legacy database technology platforms (e.g., Oracle, DB2, Sybase, etc.)
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Strong understanding of database architectures, distributed systems, and cloud computing concepts, with experience in distributed systems design, including scalability, fault tolerance, and consistency models
  • Experience in performance tuning, scalability, and high availability designs for large-scale database implementations
  • Familiarity with modern application development frameworks and current proficiency with at least one of: Java, C#, Python
  • Bachelor's or advanced degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent experience

Additional Requirements

  • There are two components to the role: in-person engagements and the ability to scale your influence through published content. We are looking for someone that can travel extensively (up to 50% of the…

jobs.title: Field Marketing Specialist
jobs.body:
The Regional Marketing Specialist supports the planning and execution of integrated marketing programs that drive pipeline, engagement, and revenue in the North region in AMER. Partnering closely with the North Senior Regional Marketing Manager, Sales team members, and cross-functional Marketing teams, this role helps land and scale programs that build developer and buyer engagement and accelerate deals across priority accounts.

You are a detail-oriented, execution-focused marketer who enjoys coordinating campaigns, managing logistics, and using data to continuously improve program performance.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Austin, Chicago, Indianapolis, or Minneapolis for our hybrid working model. 

Key Responsibilities

Campaign & Program Execution

  • Support execution of regional marketing programs (e.g., Developer Days, Architect Days, design reviews, product webinars, third-party events, partner activities)
  • Coordinate program logistics, including invites, landing pages, email promotions, audience lists, and follow-up workflows in partnership with Marketing Operations
  • Localize global campaigns and assets for the region, ensuring relevance to local industries and personas

Sales & Regional Alignment

  • Work closely with sales team members to align programs to regional priorities, target accounts, and routes to revenue
  • Help maintain and communicate a regional marketing calendar, so GTM teams understand near-term and upcoming programs
  • Provide Sales with campaign overviews, talking points, and follow-up content to maximize conversion from events and campaigns

Data, Reporting & Insights

  • Pull and organize campaign and regional performance data (e.g., registrations, attendees, MQLs, opportunities, pipeline) from Tableau, CRM, and marketing dashboards to support monthly/quarterly reviews
  • Help analyze trends by program, account segment, and sub-region to identify what’s working and where to iterate
  • Maintain accurate tagging and documentation to measure programs consistently across the region

Stakeholder Communication & Enablement

  • Draft internal program briefs, Slack updates, newsletters, and recaps to keep GTM stakeholders informed of marketing plans and results
  • Partner with design, content, DevRel, and events teams to ensure assets and experiences align with brand and program goals
  • Capture and share best practices and learnings from programs that can be replicated across regions

Operational Excellence

  • Support regional planning cycles by helping with retrospective performance pulls, Key Growth account analysis, and draft slides for regional marketing plan…

jobs.title: Financial Planning & Analysis Manager
jobs.body:
MongoDB is seeking a Manager, Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) as the dedicated Finance business partner to the International Product & Technology (P&T) leadership team. You will be responsible for financial planning, forecasting, reporting, and analyzing aspects of key business decisions and resource planning. The Manager is expected to make significant contributions to the success of MongoDB by enabling executives and team leaders to make better decisions and will work closely with them to evaluate, plan, execute, and measure strategic business initiatives. Furthermore, the manager will own special projects such as software capitalization and be the driving force behind our international expansion into Ireland and India. 

This position requires an adaptable self-starter who is able to work comfortably and communicate clearly with stakeholders across the organization, progress tasks from start to finish, and enjoys working in a fast-paced, challenging environment.

You will own putting together a monthly BVA for the CTO and his executive leadership team, and improve upon existing reporting
  • Forecast Coordination & Modelling: Build and maintain detailed financial and operating models to support your stakeholders in all aspects of their budget with a strong focus on headcount planning and optimization

  • Perform financial month-end close activities, including headcount reconciliations, accrual reviews, and budget vs. actuals analysis and reporting which include leading the Ireland Grant, Credit monthly close process 
  • Operational Effectiveness: Improve and scale business, operational and financial processes understood by all important partners, including standardizing and automating reports and improving how we leverage internal systems

What You'll Need:
